actionable_feedback: Correct the sensitivity testing implementation: in-sheet, apply +/-5% perturbations to each input driver (scope_weight, last_used_hours, rotation_score) individually and in combination, recompute derived normalized drivers and observed_risk, and record resulting class changes.
Add explicit expert-review flagging logic: add columns that mark rows requiring manual review when any perturbation changes class_code (baseline vs any perturbed-case).

Make remediation recommendations explicit and verifiable: add a Remediation Recommendations table (both in the .xlsx Remediation sheet and the runbook) listing each recommended option combination, the resulting class outcomes per record, total_cost_usd, and confirmation that total_cost_usd <= remediation_budget_usd. Filter out or flag combinations exceeding budget.

Increase artifact inspectability: include CSV exports of key sheets (Model, Sensitivity, Remediation) and a plaintext 'formula_audit.txt' or a runbook appendices sheet that lists the exact in-sheet formulas used (so graders without openpyxl can verify formulas and logic).

Untruncate and expand the runbook preview: ensure containment_runbook.md includes full sensitivity tables and the remediation recommendation output (not truncated) and reference the exact cell ranges in the workbook for traceability.

Fix grader/verifier metadata inconsistencies: when re-running grading, ensure the grader's JSON output consistently maps rubric item scores to the final_score (document the scoring mapping) and that 'passed'/'failed_criteria' and 'passed_criteria' are not contradictory.
Re-run the grading/verifier after implementing the above; attach updated stdout/stderr/agent_trace so verifiers can re-evaluate whether the corrections address rubric failure modes.
artifact_specific_comments: secret_rotation_priority.xlsx: workbook reportedly contains Model, Sensitivity, and Remediation sheets with formulas, but environment lacked openpyxl so grader could not parse .xlsx; include CSV/formula audit to aid verification.
containment_runbook.md: documents assumptions and formulas clearly but preview is truncated and sensitivity section shows observed_risk multiplied by 0.95/1.05 rather than input-driver perturbations.
failed_or_weak_rubric_items: {'rubric_item_id': 'ri_1', 'issue': 'Sensitivity testing implemented incorrectly (applied to observed_risk instead of input drivers) and remediation recommendation visibility insufficient for external verification.'}
failed_rubric_items: {'rubric_item_id': 'ri_1', 'issue': 'Sensitivity testing implemented incorrectly (applied to observed_risk instead of input drivers) and remediation recommendation visibility insufficient for external verification.'}
feedback_summary: Work artifacts meet the artifact-contract (files present and documented) but contain two classes of problems: (1) a substantive correctness gap in sensitivity testing (sensitivity was applied to observed_risk rather than to the input drivers: scope_weight, last_used_hours, rotation_score) which violates the rubric's sensitivity requirement; and (2) verifier-detected metadata/internal-consistency problems in the grader outputs (contradictory pass/fail flags and mismatch between rubric item scores and final_score) that make the reported final score and pass status unreliable. Additionally, graders could not programmatically open the .xlsx in the environment; supply a verifier-friendly alternative (CSV or plaintext formula audit) to enable robust external verification.

recommended_fixes: Re-implement sensitivity tests to perturb input drivers in-sheet and document perturbation rows and resulting class-change flags.
Add CSV exports and plaintext formula audit sheet so graders without openpyxl can inspect formulas and outputs.
Produce an untruncated runbook (containment_runbook.md) that includes full sensitivity results and remediation recommendation outputs and references specific cell ranges.
Correct grader output generation so rubric item scores, passed/failed_criteria, and final_score are consistent and document the mapping from rubric items to final semantic score.

verifier_issues.description: Grader output contains contradictory pass/fail and rubric score -> final_score inconsistencies; this must be resolved before final score can be trusted.
verifier_issues.title: Internal consistency errors in grader result

verifier_issues.description: Grader could not parse .xlsx programmatically and runbook preview was truncated; include CSV/formula audit and full runbook to help verification.
verifier_issues.title: openpyxl unavailable & truncated runbook preview

verifier_issues.description: Sensitivity was applied to observed_risk directly-update workbook to perturb input drivers as required by rubric.
verifier_issues.title: Sensitivity implemented incorrectly
